method: width-preserving random-gap permutation test on horizontal pitch-CV (E079 qualification). Observed center-to-center pitch-CV is compared against a null that preserves each glyph's actual width and the row's center-span, randomizing ONLY the inter-glyph gap allocation. Deliberate even spacing = observed pitch-CV significantly BELOW the width-preserving null.
key_findings: E079's horizontal pitch-regularity is WIDTH-DRIVEN, not deliberate even spacing. Observed median pitch-CV (0.338) is ABOVE the width-preserving random-gap null (0.271), perm_p=1.0, ratio 1.25 — the test fails to reject the null of deliberate spacing; no deliberate gap regularity was detected once widths were held fixed. This does NOT establish that observed gaps are less regular than random, only that no deliberate regularity was detected.

The result is uniform across all three qualifying sites: Haghia Triada (S_obs 0.381 vs null 0.292, p=1.0), Khania (0.291 vs 0.230, p=1.0), Zakros (0.346 vs 0.272, p=1.0). Zero sites show observed pitch-CV below the width-preserving null.

Positive control PASSED cleanly: the machinery detects synthetic deliberate even-spacing with detect_p=0.005 and power_est=1.0 over 15 reps, and produces zero false positives (rate 0.0) on width-only random-gap synthetic docs. The negative result is therefore a genuine property of the corpus, not a machinery failure.

E079's original pitch-CV (0.329) vs random-composition null (0.743) is fully explained by the combination of (i) fairly uniform glyph widths (E076) and (ii) the row center-span constraint: once widths and span are held fixed, even RANDOM gap allocation yields pitch-CV ~0.27, which is already at or below the observed 0.338.
The inter-glyph GAPS themselves carry no detectable deliberate regularity; any apparent evenness of pitch is a geometric consequence of uniform glyph widths filling a bounded row span.

E082's MACHINERY_UNINFORMATIVE outcome is now resolved: the width-preserving null that E082's PC could not separate IS separable by this machinery (PC power 1.0), and the data fall decisively on the width-driven side.
successor_hypotheses: E084 (L2): Test whether glyph WIDTHS themselves are more uniform within rows than a corpus-shuffled null — i.e., is the width-uniformity that drives the pitch-regularity itself a deliberate scribal normalization, or an intrinsic property of the sign inventory?

E085 (L2): Quantify the row center-span constraint — does the bounded writing surface (line length) mechanically force pitch-CV down regardless of gap intent? Decompose pitch-CV into width-CV and gap-CV contributions per row.
E086 (L2): Re-examine VERTICAL spacing (between rows) with the same width/height-preserving null to test whether vertical regularity is deliberate where horizontal is not.

E087 (L2): Per-sign width analysis — do specific high-frequency signs have especially uniform widths that would disproportionately regularize pitch? (L2 geometry only, aggregated over sign classes, not semantics.)

E088 (L2): Test for deliberate spacing at the level of word/divider groups rather than individual glyphs — dividers were excluded here; grouping by divider-delimited units may reveal intentional spacing at a coarser granularity.

E089 (L2): Cross-script comparison — apply the identical width-preserving null to an external corpus (e.g., later Greek epigraphy or a non-script geometric baseline) to DESCRIBE how width-driven pitch-CV behaves elsewhere. NOTE: no ground-truth label of deliberateness is available for any corpus, so this is exploratory description only — it cannot establish a reference distribution or validate against known-deliberate vs known-casual writing, and any cross-corpus numeric differences are uninterpretable as evidence of deliberateness.
non_circular: Width-preserving null reuses each glyph's actual width and the row center-span; only gap allocation is randomized, isolating gap-regularity from width-regularity. No sign identities used; bbox geometry only (left, width, y-center, height). E079's original random-composition null is replaced by a stricter width-matched null, so any signal here is NOT explainable by E079's mechanism.
